title: [FOLDED → OOM-0036] Abort: negative refcount on a freed `str` (`tuple_dealloc`, `tupleobject.c:277`)
notes: RETIRED 2026-06-25 — folded into OOM-0036. rr reverse-execution of the repro (`_pyrepl.utils.disp_str(mixed-control/high str)` under the set_nomemory sweep) traced the over-decref'd victim: it is a `str` (a display segment), NOT a MemoryError as this report originally claimed. disp_str builds a list of display segments via list.append; the victim str is appended (self had ob_size=4), the grow fails under OOM (Py_DECREF(newitem==victim)@listobject.c:531 <- `_PyList_AppendTakeRefListResize` <- `_CALL_LIST_APPEND` gen 3981), and the leftover stolen stackref is closed again by exception_unwind (gen 13857) -> double-free = OOM-0036 (python/cpython#151818). The negref is then detected later when a tuple/list dealloc cascade (list_dealloc:567 -> subtype_dealloc:2876 -> tuple_dealloc:277, the segment tuples) Py_XDECREFs the already-freed str. So tuple_dealloc/list_dealloc/subtype_dealloc are only DETECTOR sites. The 'load-bearing mixed-control/high arg' just produces enough list.append segments to land a resize-failure in the sweep window; a single char makes too few segments. Fourth OOM-0036 face found via rr (after OOM-0005/0041/0033) -- same _CALL_LIST_APPEND root, different stdlib path. Gen scripts skip status==folded (keys dropped). DEDUP: OOM-0029's unique keys (tuple_dealloc:277, listobject.c:567) drop; the generic dealloc-cascade frames it shared (subtype_dealloc, list_dealloc, typeobject.c:2876) remain under their real owners (OOM-0023 subtype_dealloc+msg, OOM-0004 list_dealloc+clear_freelist, OOM-0030 subtype_dealloc+unicode_is_singleton) -- a tuple_dealloc negref now resolves ambiguous-known or oomNEW -> rr-triage (almost always OOM-0036). Gist 10e0fdaf should be marked superseded (flagged to maintainer). id NOT reused.
